title: Turn Your AI Agent Into an Autonomous GitHub Contributor
description: Transform a sandboxed AI agent into a professional GitHub contributor that can autonomously make code contributions and pull requests.

cards.title: Welcome to AI Agent GitHub Integration
cards.body: You'll learn how to transform your sandboxed AI agent into a professional GitHub contributor that can autonomously write code, create repositories, and make pull requests. This builds on having an existing AI agent running on a GCP virtual machine.

cards.title: Why Your Agent Needs Its Own GitHub
cards.body: In open source development, GitHub is your resume. We'll create a separate GitHub account for your agent to establish proper identity and professional boundaries.

analogy.text: Just like you wouldn't share your personal social media with a colleague, your agent needs its own professional identity

bullets: Separate identity prevents confusion about who wrote what code
Builds trust in the open source community
Creates a clear audit trail for AI contributions

cards.title: Create Your Agent's GitHub Account
cards.body: Set up a dedicated GitHub account for your AI agent. Use a separate email address and clearly identify it as an AI agent in the profile.
bullets: Use a dedicated email address for the agent
Add a note in the profile that this is an AI agent
Choose a username that reflects its AI nature
cards.warn: Always disclose that this is an AI agent account to maintain transparency in the open source community

source.author: bri
source.description: Creating the agent's GitHub account and setting up the profile

cards.title: Required Tools for GitHub Integration
cards.body: Your agent needs two essential tools installed on its GCP virtual machine to interact with GitHub repositories.
bullets: GitHub CLI: Official tool for GitHub operations
cards.safe: Your agent can install these tools automatically - you just need to tell it what you need

cards.title: Install Git and GitHub CLI
cards.body: Tell your Telegram bot to install the necessary tools on your GCP virtual machine. The agent will handle all the technical installation steps.
cards.code: Install Git and GitHub CLI on the virtual machine
cards.codeCaption: Send this request to your agent via Telegram

source.author: bri
source.description: Installing Git and GitHub CLI through the Telegram bot

cards.title: Quick Check
cards.question: Why do we install both Git and GitHub CLI on the virtual machine?

options.text: Git for version control, GitHub CLI for GitHub-specific operations
They're the same tool with different names
Only GitHub CLI is needed, Git is optional
Git is for local files, GitHub CLI is for remote files

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Git handles version control while GitHub CLI provides direct GitHub integration.
cards.wrongFeedback: Git and GitHub CLI serve different but complementary purposes for GitHub workflow.

cards.title: Personal Access Tokens Explained
cards.body: A Personal Access Token (PAT) is like a password that gives your agent permission to access GitHub on behalf of the account.

analogy.text: Think of it like giving someone a house key - you can give them full access or limit which rooms they can enter

bullets: Full access for the agent's own account
Restricted access if connecting to your personal account
Can be revoked or updated anytime

cards.title: Generate a Personal Access Token
cards.body: Create a PAT to give your agent access to its GitHub account. Since this is the agent's own account, we'll grant full access.
bullets: Go to Settings → Developer Settings → Personal Access Tokens → Tokens Classic
Select all permissions (since it's the agent's own account)
cards.warn: The token will only be shown once - copy it immediately and keep it secure

source.author: bri
source.description: Generating a Personal Access Token with full permissions

cards.title: Configure Agent's GitHub Credentials
cards.body: Send your agent all the information it needs to connect to GitHub in one message. Include the PAT, username, email, and author details.
cards.code:
Connect GitHub account:
- Token: [YOUR_PAT_HERE]
- Username: [agent-username]
- Email: [agent-email@example.com]
- Author name: [Agent Name]
- Committer name: [Agent Name]
cards.codeCaption: Replace the bracketed values with your agent's actual information
cards.safe: The agent will securely configure git credentials on the virtual machine

cards.title: Agent Connected to GitHub!
cards.body: Your AI agent now has its own GitHub identity and can push code to repositories. Next, we'll make it autonomous and professional.

cards.title: Defining Your Agent's Identity
cards.body: A professional AI contributor needs a clear identity and focus area. This helps it make meaningful contributions to the right projects.
bullets: Choose a specific ecosystem (like Solana, React, Python)
Define the types of projects it should work on
Set clear boundaries for what it should and shouldn't do

analogy.text: Like hiring a specialist consultant - they're most valuable when they focus on their expertise

cards.title: Set Your Agent's Professional Identity
cards.body: Define what kind of software engineer your agent should be. Choose an ecosystem and specialization that aligns with your work.
cards.code:
I want you to be a software engineer focused on the [ECOSYSTEM] ecosystem. Focus on:
- [Specific technology 1]
- [Specific technology 2]
- [Type of projects]
cards.codeCaption: Customize this template for your agent's focus area

source.author: bri
source.description: Setting the agent's identity as a Solana ecosystem developer

cards.title: Pre-Commit Checklist for Quality
cards.body: Professional developers follow quality checks before committing code. Your agent needs the same discipline to create trustworthy contributions.
cards.warn: Skipping quality checks can damage your agent's reputation in the open source community

cards.title: Create a Pre-Commit Workflow
cards.body: Define specific steps your agent must follow before making any code commits to ensure quality and professionalism.
cards.code:
Before committing any code:
1. Run all tests
2. Check for security vulnerabilities
3. Write clear commit messages
4. Update documentation
5. Verify code style standards

cards.title: Quality Check
cards.question: What's the most important reason for having a pre-commit checklist?

options.text: To make the agent work slower and more carefully
To ensure contributions are professional and trustworthy
To make the code look more complex
To avoid using GitHub's built-in features

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Quality checks build trust and credibility in the open source community.
cards.wrongFeedback: Pre-commit checklists are about maintaining professional standards and building trust.

cards.title: GitHub Etiquette for AI Agents
cards.body: Your agent needs to follow professional GitHub etiquette to successfully contribute to open source projects and get pull requests merged.
bullets: Always read project contribution guidelines first
Write clear, helpful pull request descriptions
cards.safe: Following proper etiquette increases the chances of your agent's contributions being accepted

cards.title: Define GitHub Etiquette Rules
cards.body: Set clear professional standards for how your agent should behave when contributing to GitHub repositories.
cards.code:
GitHub Etiquette Rules:
- Read CONTRIBUTING.md before making changes
- Write detailed PR descriptions
- Be respectful in all interactions
- Follow project coding standards
- Test changes thoroughly
- Respond promptly to feedback
cards.codeCaption: Send these rules to your agent to ensure professional behavior

source.author: bri
source.description: Setting up GitHub etiquette rules for professional contributions

cards.title: Push Your First Repository
cards.body: Have your agent create its first public repository by pushing existing code (like the trading bot from previous tutorials) with proper documentation.
cards.safe: This creates a foundation for your agent's GitHub profile and demonstrates its capabilities

cards.title: Making It Autonomous
cards.body: The final step is setting up your agent to work continuously and autonomously, contributing to projects on a regular schedule.
bullets: Set up a cron schedule for regular activity
Define target projects to contribute to
Monitor and adjust performance over time

analogy.text: Like having a dedicated team member who works around the clock on open source contributions

cards.title: Set Up Autonomous Operation
cards.body: Configure your agent to run autonomously with scheduled contributions and clear goals.
cards.code:
Set up autonomous GitHub contributions:
- Run daily at 9 AM
- Target: 5 meaningful contributions per month
- Focus on [your ecosystem] projects
- Follow all quality and etiquette guidelines
- Report weekly progress
cards.codeCaption: Adjust the schedule and goals based on your preferences
cards.warn: Start with conservative goals and increase as your agent proves reliable

cards.title: Final Check
cards.question: What makes an AI agent's GitHub contributions valuable to the open source community?

options.text: The quantity of commits per day
Professional behavior, quality code, and following project guidelines
Using the most advanced AI model available
Making changes to as many repositories as possible

cards.correctFeedback: Perfect! Quality and professionalism matter more than quantity in open source.
cards.wrongFeedback: Focus on quality contributions and professional behavior rather than just volume.

cards.title: Your AI Agent is Now a GitHub Contributor!
cards.body: You've successfully transformed your AI agent into an autonomous GitHub contributor with its own identity, professional standards, and quality workflows. It can now contribute to open source projects and build a legitimate development portfolio.
